violation.name: com/google/common/collect/Maps.newConcurrentMap:()Ljava/util/concurrent/ConcurrentMap;
violation.comment: Prefer java.util.concurrent.ConcurrentHashMap<>() or customize the map using com.google.common.collect.MapMaker

violation.name: com/google/common/io/Files.append:(Ljava/lang/CharSequence;Ljava/io/File;Ljava/nio/charset/Charset;)V
violation.comment: Prefer java.nio.file.Files.writeString(Path, CharSequence, Charset, StandardOpenOption.CREATE, StandardOpenOption.APPEND)

violation.name: com/google/common/primitives/Ints.checkedCast:(J)I
violation.comment: Prefer java.lang.Math.toIntExact(long); note that it throws an ArithmeticException instead of an IllegalArgumentException

violation.name: com/google/common/math/IntMath.mod:(II)I
violation.comment: Prefer java.lang.Math.floorMod(int, int); note that IntMath.mod throws for non-positive m while Math.floorMod accepts any nonzero modulus

violation.name: com/google/common/math/LongMath.mod:(JJ)J
violation.comment: Prefer java.lang.Math.floorMod(long, long); note that LongMath.mod throws for non-positive m while Math.floorMod accepts any nonzero modulus

violation.name: java/util/Collections.singletonList:(Ljava/lang/Object;)Ljava/util/List;
violation.comment: Prefer java.util.List.of(Object) which rejects null elements

violation.name: java/util/Collections.singleton:(Ljava/lang/Object;)Ljava/util/Set;
violation.comment: Prefer java.util.Set.of(Object) which rejects null elements

violation.name: java/util/Collections.singletonMap:(Ljava/lang/Object;Ljava/lang/Object;)Ljava/util/Map;
violation.comment: Prefer java.util.Map.of(Object, Object) which rejects null keys and values

violation.name: org/apache/commons/codec/binary/Base64.decodeBase64:(Ljava/lang/String;)[B
violation.comment: Prefer java.util.Base64.Decoder.decode(String); note that it rejects line breaks and other non-Base64 characters which decodeBase64 ignores; use Base64.getMimeDecoder() for lenient decoding

violation.name: org/apache/commons/io/FileUtils.openOutputStream:(Ljava/io/File;)Ljava/io/FileOutputStream;
violation.comment: Prefer java.nio.file.Files.newOutputStream(Path, OpenOption...); note that openOutputStream creates missing parent directories

violation.name: org/apache/commons/io/FileUtils.openOutputStream:(Ljava/io/File;Z)Ljava/io/FileOutputStream;
violation.comment: Prefer java.nio.file.Files.newOutputStream(Path, OpenOption...); note that openOutputStream creates missing parent directories

violation.name: org/apache/commons/io/FileUtils.contentEquals:(Ljava/io/File;Ljava/io/File;)Z
violation.comment: Prefer java.nio.file.Files.mismatch(Path, Path) == -1; note that mismatch throws NoSuchFileException for missing files while contentEquals returns true when both are missing

violation.name: sun/misc/BASE64Decoder.decodeBuffer:(Ljava/lang/String;)[B
violation.comment: Prefer java.util.Base64.Decoder.decode(String); note that it rejects line breaks and other non-Base64 characters which BASE64Decoder ignores; use Base64.getMimeDecoder() for lenient decoding

violation.name: sun/misc/BASE64Encoder.encode:([B)Ljava/lang/String;
violation.comment: Prefer java.util.Base64.Encoder.encodeToString(byte[]); note that it does not emit line breaks like BASE64Encoder; use Base64.getMimeEncoder() to wrap lines

violation.name: com/google/common/base/Preconditions.checkNotNull:(Ljava/lang/Object;Ljava/lang/Object;)Ljava/lang/Object;
violation.comment: Prefer java.util.Objects.requireNonNull(Object, String) or java.util.Objects.requireNonNull(Object, Supplier<String>)

violation.name: java/util/stream/Collectors.toUnmodifiableList:()Ljava/util/stream/Collector;
violation.comment: Prefer java.util.stream.Stream.toList(); note that Stream.toList() permits null elements while toUnmodifiableList() rejects them with NullPointerException

violation.name: com/google/common/collect/Iterables.concat:(Ljava/lang/Iterable;Ljava/lang/Iterable;Ljava/lang/Iterable;Ljava/lang/Iterable;)Ljava/lang/Iterable;
violation.comment: Prefer Stream.of(a, b, c, d).flatMap(s -> s)

violation.name: org/apache/commons/lang3/ObjectUtils.firstNonNull:([Ljava/lang/Object;)Ljava/lang/Object;
violation.comment: Prefer java.util.Objects.requireNonNullElse(Object, Object); note that it accepts only two arguments and throws NullPointerException when both are null, while ObjectUtils.firstNonNull returns null

violation.name: org/apache/commons/lang3/ObjectUtils.defaultIfNull:(Ljava/lang/Object;Ljava/lang/Object;)Ljava/lang/Object;
violation.comment: Prefer java.util.Objects.requireNonNullElse(Object, Object); note that it throws NullPointerException when both arguments are null, while ObjectUtils.defaultIfNull returns null

violation.name: org/apache/commons/lang3/Validate.notNull:(Ljava/lang/Object;Ljava/lang/String;[Ljava/lang/Object;)Ljava/lang/Object;
violation.comment: Prefer java.util.Objects.requireNonNull(Object, String); note that requireNonNull uses the message literally while Validate.notNull formats it via String.format with the trailing arguments

violation.name: com/google/common/base/Joiner.join:(Ljava/lang/Iterable;)Ljava/lang/String;
violation.comment: Prefer java.lang.String.join or use java.util.stream.Stream with java.util.stream.Collectors.joining

violation.name: com/google/common/base/Joiner.join:(Ljava/util/Iterator;)Ljava/lang/String;
violation.comment: Prefer java.lang.String.join or use java.util.stream.Stream with java.util.stream.Collectors.joining

violation.name: com/google/common/base/Joiner.join:([Ljava/lang/Object;)Ljava/lang/String;
violation.comment: Prefer java.lang.String.join or use java.util.stream.Stream with java.util.stream.Collectors.joining

violation.name: com/google/common/base/Joiner.join:(Ljava/lang/Object;Ljava/lang/Object;[Ljava/lang/Object;)Ljava/lang/String;
violation.comment: Prefer java.lang.String.join or use java.util.stream.Stream with java.util.stream.Collectors.joining

violation.name: org/apache/commons/lang3/StringUtils.join:(Ljava/lang/Iterable;C)Ljava/lang/String;
violation.comment: Prefer java.lang.String.join or use java.util.stream.Stream with java.util.stream.Collectors.joining

violation.name: org/apache/commons/lang3/StringUtils.join:(Ljava/lang/Iterable;Ljava/lang/String;)Ljava/lang/String;
violation.comment: Prefer java.lang.String.join or use java.util.stream.Stream with java.util.stream.Collectors.joining

violation.name: org/apache/commons/lang3/StringUtils.join:(Ljava/util/Iterator;C)Ljava/lang/String;
violation.comment: Prefer java.lang.String.join or use java.util.stream.Stream with java.util.stream.Collectors.joining

violation.name: org/apache/commons/lang3/StringUtils.join:(Ljava/util/Iterator;Ljava/lang/String;)Ljava/lang/String;
violation.comment: Prefer java.lang.String.join or use java.util.stream.Stream with java.util.stream.Collectors.joining

violation.name: org/apache/commons/lang3/StringUtils.join:([Ljava/lang/Object;C)Ljava/lang/String;
violation.comment: Prefer java.lang.String.join or use java.util.stream.Stream with java.util.stream.Collectors.joining

violation.name: org/apache/commons/lang3/StringUtils.join:([Ljava/lang/Object;Ljava/lang/String;)Ljava/lang/String;
violation.comment: Prefer java.lang.String.join or use java.util.stream.Stream with java.util.stream.Collectors.joining

violation.name: org/apache/commons/codec/binary/Base64.encodeBase64Chunked:([B)[B
violation.comment: Prefer java.util.Base64.getMimeEncoder().encode(byte[]); note that it omits the trailing line break which encodeBase64Chunked appends

violation.name: org/apache/commons/codec/binary/Base64.decodeBase64:([B)[B
violation.comment: Prefer java.util.Base64.Decoder; note that it rejects line breaks and other non-Base64 characters which decodeBase64 ignores; use Base64.getMimeDecoder() for lenient decoding

violation.name: java/lang/Runtime.exec:(Ljava/lang/String;[Ljava/lang/String;Ljava/io/File;)Ljava/lang/Process;
violation.comment: Prefer java.lang.Runtime.exec(String[], String[], File) or java.lang.ProcessBuilder

violation.name: java/net/URLDecoder.decode:(Ljava/lang/String;)Ljava/lang/String;
violation.comment: Prefer java.net.URLDecoder.decode(String, String) with an explicit charset

violation.name: java/net/URLEncoder.encode:(Ljava/lang/String;)Ljava/lang/String;
violation.comment: Prefer java.net.URLEncoder.encode(String, String) with an explicit charset

violation.name: org/bouncycastle/util/encoders/Base64.decode:(Ljava/lang/String;)[B
violation.comment: Prefer java.util.Base64.Decoder.decode(String); note that it rejects line breaks and other whitespace which decode ignores; use Base64.getMimeDecoder() for lenient decoding

violation.name: org/bouncycastle/util/encoders/Base64.decode:([B)[B
violation.comment: Prefer java.util.Base64.Decoder; note that it rejects line breaks and other whitespace which decode ignores; use Base64.getMimeDecoder() for lenient decoding

violation.name: org/bouncycastle/util/encoders/Hex.toHexString:([BII)Ljava/lang/String;
violation.comment: Prefer java.util.HexFormat.of().formatHex(byte[], int, int); note that formatHex takes an exclusive to index rather than a length

violation.name: org/bouncycastle/util/encoders/Hex.decode:(Ljava/lang/String;)[B
violation.comment: Prefer java.util.HexFormat.of().parseHex(String); note that parseHex rejects whitespace which decode ignores

violation.name: org/bouncycastle/util/encoders/Hex.decode:([B)[B
violation.comment: Prefer java.util.HexFormat.of().parseHex(String); note that parseHex rejects whitespace which decode ignores
